threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still use the Tube map like it’s gospel, not realizing the bus is often quicker, especially after a strike.
Last night on the 59, a couple had a full-on argument over which kebab shop was best. The whole bus chimed in, like an unexpected late-night panel show.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I realized I lived in Neukölln? When I saw a street performer juggling flaming sticks while a dog listened intently—only in Berlin.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: You know those random street performances in eclectic Kreuzberg? They often have more enthusiastic crowds than any concert hall. Truly unique vibe.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: Only a true local would know to bring a spare layer for the morning fog, but ditch it by 2 PM when the sun's roasting.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy in a full suit, holding an umbrella, waiting for Muni in the rain while sipping coffee. Classic SF dedication to style no matter the chaos.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think Gràcia is all about the parks, but they miss the best tapas tucked away on side streets, where locals gather after work.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell a recent arrival by how they confuse 'paseo' with 'paseo marítimo' when looking for the beach—locals just call it 'la playa'.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: When you ask for directions to the nearest tube and get a blank stare—everyone here knows it’s bus or bike all the way.
Heard a guy on the 3 at 2 AM claiming he’s a ‘night bus philosopher’—kept quoting obscure poets while everyone else was half-asleep. Classic Brixton.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: When I saw a toddler in a stroller munching on a döner while waiting for the bus, I realized this is home. Only in Neukölln.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Berlin do you see a spontaneous party break out at 3 AM in an abandoned train station, complete with DJs and dance circles. It’s wild.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can spot a true local by their disdain for tourists stopping dead in the crosswalk just to snap a pic of a taco truck.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught a Muni bus today that stopped mid-traffic because a pigeon decided it needed a nap on the tracks. Classic SF chaos.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists often think Gràcia is just about hip bars, but it's the local bakeries at dawn where the real magic happens—fresh pa de pessic that’ll knock your socks off.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can always tell by the way they mispronounce 'Gràcia' – if they say 'Gra-sia,' they’re still figuring it out.
